text: LinkedIn connection request note — hard 300 character limit. State your role and the job you're applying for. Name one real project or technology. End with a direct ask to connect or chat. No buzzwords, no emojis, no 'I am excited'. Professional but human.

LinkedIn InMail structure: 3 short paragraphs under 150 words. P1: who you are and the role. P2: one specific proof from your resume that matches the JD. P3: clear ask — 5-minute call or to review your application. Never start with 'I hope this message finds you well.'

Cold job application email: 3 paragraphs, under 100 words. P1: introduce yourself and state the exact role. P2: 1-2 core technologies or projects that align with JD main requirements — factual and brief. P3: clean call to action — ask for a 5-minute call or to review attached resume.

Follow-up email after applying: send 5-7 business days after submission. 2 short paragraphs. P1: reference your application — role, date, and one strong qualifier from your resume. P2: restate interest with one specific reason tied to the company's work. Keep it under 80 words.

Referral request message: be direct about what you want — a referral for a specific role. Provide the job link and your resume. Mention one specific shared connection or reason you're reaching out to them. Make it easy: tell them exactly what to do. Keep it under 100 words.

Interview thank-you email: send within 24 hours. Reference one specific topic discussed in the interview. Restate your interest and one concrete thing you bring to the role. Keep it under 100 words. Never use: 'I wanted to circle back', 'per my last email', 'as I mentioned'.

HR communication tone rules: write like a professional, not a job board template. Short sentences. Active voice. Specific details beat general enthusiasm. Never use: passionate, excited, thrilled, eager, leverage, synergy, team player, quick learner, go-getter, results-driven, motivated.

Email subject lines that get opened: 'Application for [Role] — [Name]', '[Name] — [Role] at [Company] (Referral from [Name])', 'Following up on [Role] application — [Name]'. Always include the exact role title. Never use: 'Exciting opportunity', 'Quick question', 'Checking in'.
